_comment: Sprint 062 — additions to the flow scheduler. The Odin background scheduler currently supports ONLY 'every_Nm' interval schedules (see crates/odin/src/flow_scheduler.rs parse_simple_interval). Full cron expressions (e.g. '0 3 * * *' = daily at 03:00) are NOT yet parsed; the closest supported schedule is 'every_1440m' (every 24h from the scheduler's reference epoch).
_note_on_daily_03_00: True 'daily at 03:00 local' requires adding a full cron parser (e.g. the `cron` crate) and an absolute-time check path. Until then, dream_self_improvement uses 'every_1440m' — it will fire once per day but the phase is tied to the Odin start time, not local wall-clock. A complementary trigger is the session-init hook in the VS Code extension (hookManager.ts SessionStart) which POSTs /api/flows/dream_self_improvement/run at session start so the user always has fresh suggestions even if the cron phase drifts.

_planned_upgrade.feature: parse and evaluate real cron expressions (seconds-resolution)
_planned_upgrade.schema_change: FlowTrigger::Cron.schedule remains a String; flow_scheduler.rs gains a parse_cron branch that falls back to parse_simple_interval

new_cron_entries.intended_wall_clock: 03:00 local (drifts with Odin restart time — see _note_on_daily_03_00)

session_init_triggers.source: VS Code extension hookManager SessionStart hook
session_init_triggers.rationale: Guarantees a fresh batch of improvement candidates at every session start regardless of cron drift.
